title: Two Sum
statement: Find indices of two numbers in array that add up to target.
hint: Use hash map: check if target-num exists

title: Valid Parentheses
statement: Check if string of brackets is valid.
hint: Use stack, push open, pop and match close

title: Fibonacci Memoized
statement: Generate Fibonacci efficiently using memoization.

title: Anagram Groups
statement: Group anagrams from [eat,tea,tan,ate,nat,bat].
hint: Use dict with sorted(word) as key
solution:
from collections import defaultdict
words=["eat","tea","tan","ate","nat","bat"]
d=defaultdict(list)
for w in words:
    d[tuple(sorted(w))].append(w)
print(list(d.values()))
optimized:
from collections import defaultdict
d=defaultdict(list)
[d[tuple(sorted(w))].append(w) for w in["eat","tea","tan","ate","nat","bat"]]
print(list(d.values()))

title: Merge Intervals
statement: Merge overlapping intervals [[1,3],[2,6],[8,10],[15,18]].
hint: Sort, then merge if current.start <= prev.end

title: Longest Common Prefix
statement: Find longest common prefix of [flower,flow,flight].
hint: Compare char by char across all strings

title: Maximum Subarray
statement: Find maximum sum subarray of [-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1,-5,4].
solution:
def max_sub(nums):
    cur=mx=nums[0]
    for n in nums[1:]:
        cur=max(n,cur+n)
        mx=max(mx,cur)
    return mx
print(max_sub([-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1,-5,4]))

title: Climbing Stairs
statement: Count ways to climb n=10 stairs (1 or 2 steps at a time).
solution:
def climb(n):
    if n<=2:return n
    a,b=1,2
    for _ in range(3,n+1):
        a,b=b,a+b
    return b
print(climb(10))
optimized:
def climb(n):
    a,b=1,2
    for _ in range(n-2):a,b=b,a+b
    return b if n>1 else 1
print(climb(10))

title: LRU Cache
statement: Implement LRU Cache with get and put in O(1).
input_example: capacity=2, put(1,1), put(2,2), get(1), put(3,3), get(2)

title: Word Frequency Top K
statement: Find top 3 most frequent words in a paragraph.
input_example: the cat sat on the mat the cat
solution:
from collections import Counter
text="the cat sat on the mat the cat"
print(Counter(text.split()).most_common(3))
optimized: from collections import Counter;print(Counter("the cat sat on the mat the cat".split()).most_common(3))

title: Spiral Matrix
statement: Return elements of 3x3 matrix in spiral order.
solution:
def spiral(matrix):
    result=[]
    while matrix:
        result+=matrix.pop(0)
        matrix=[list(x) for x in zip(*matrix)][::-1]
    return result
print(spiral([[1,2,3],[4,5,6],[7,8,9]]))
optimized:
def spiral(m):
    r=[]
    while m:r+=m.pop(0);m=[list(x)for x in zip(*m)][::-1]
    return r
print(spiral([[1,2,3],[4,5,6],[7,8,9]]))

hint: Push to stack on [, pop and multiply on ]

title: Product Except Self
statement: Return array where each element is product of all others. No division.
solution:
def product_except_self(nums):
    n=len(nums);res=[1]*n
    left=1
    for i in range(n):res[i]=left;left*=nums[i]
    right=1
    for i in range(n-1,-1,-1):res[i]*=right;right*=nums[i]
    return res
print(product_except_self([1,2,3,4]))
optimized: Same - O(n) time, O(1) extra space

title: Trapping Rain Water
statement: Calculate water trapped in [0,1,0,2,1,0,1,3,2,1,2,1].
solution:
def trap(height):
    l,r=0,len(height)-1
    lmax=rmax=water=0
    while l<r:
        if height[l]<height[r]:
            if height[l]>=lmax:lmax=height[l]
            else:water+=lmax-height[l]
            l+=1
        else:
            if height[r]>=rmax:rmax=height[r]
            else:water+=rmax-height[r]
            r-=1
    return water
print(trap([0,1,0,2,1,0,1,3,2,1,2,1]))

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
statement: Find length of LIS in [10,9,2,5,3,7,101,18].
hint: dp[i] = max(dp[j]+1) for j<i where nums[j]<nums[i]
